items.name: IoError
items.doc:
Structured I/O error: a classified `kind` plus the originating POSIX
errno.

Returned in the `Err` arm of every `Result` produced by `Read`, `Write`,
`File`, `stdio`, and the `os.fs` helpers. Pattern-match on `kind` for
programmatic dispatch; call `description()` for a short human-readable
phrase. The convenience constructors at the bottom (`notFound()`,
`permissionDenied()`, etc.) build common kinds without spelling the enum.

# Examples

```
match File.open("missing.txt") {
    .Ok(f) => use(f),
    .Err(e) => match e.kind {
        .NotFound          => createDefault(),
        .PermissionDenied  => requestAccess(),
        _                  => log(e.description())
    }
}
```

members.name: From Code
members.doc: Builds an error from a raw POSIX errno; classifies the kind.

members.name: From Kind
members.doc: Builds an error for a categorized kind.

members.name: description
members.doc:
Returns a short human-readable phrase for the error kind. Unknown
codes yield `"unknown error"`; for full coverage use `errno()`
with a platform `strerror`.

members.name: errno
members.doc:
The raw POSIX error code. Use for programmatic dispatch when
pattern-matching on `kind` is too coarse — e.g. distinguishing
between `.Other` codes.

members.name: last
members.doc:
Snapshots the current value of the platform's `errno` thread-local.
Call immediately after a failed libc call — any other libc activity
in between can clobber the value.

items.name: IoErrorKind
items.doc:
Categorical classification of an I/O error.

`IoError` carries one of these alongside its raw `errno`. The named
variants cover the common categories applications dispatch on; everything
else falls into `.Other` carrying the original POSIX code so no
information is lost. Built from a code via `IoErrorKind.fromErrno(code:)`,
or matched directly in error-handling code:

```
match e.kind {
    .NotFound          => createDefault(),
    .PermissionDenied  => promptForElevation(),
    .Other(c)          => log("unhandled errno: " + c.toString())
}
```

members.name: AlreadyExists
members.doc: `EEXIST` — the path already exists (e.g. `O_CREAT | O_EXCL`).

members.name: BadFileDescriptor
members.doc: `EBADF` — file descriptor is invalid or closed.

members.name: BrokenPipe
members.doc: `EPIPE` — write to a pipe with no reader.

members.name: Interrupted
members.doc: `EINTR` — operation interrupted by a signal.

members.name: InvalidInput
members.doc: `EINVAL` — invalid argument to a libc call.

members.name: IoFailure
members.doc: `EIO` — generic kernel-reported I/O failure.

members.name: IsADirectory
members.doc: `EISDIR` — operation expected a file but got a directory.

members.name: NoSpaceLeft
members.doc: `ENOSPC` — no space left on device.

members.name: NotADirectory
members.doc: `ENOTDIR` — a path component is not a directory.

members.name: NotFound
members.doc: `ENOENT` — the path does not exist.

members.name: NotPermitted
members.doc: `EPERM` — operation not permitted.

members.name: Other
members.doc:
Any other POSIX errno — keeps the original code so callers can
still dispatch on the raw value.

members.name: OutOfMemory
members.doc: `ENOMEM` — kernel allocation failed.

members.name: PermissionDenied
members.doc: `EACCES` — caller lacks permission for the operation.

members.name: WouldBlock
members.doc: `EAGAIN` — non-blocking call would have blocked.

members.name: description
members.doc: Short human-readable phrase, locale-independent.

members.name: errno
members.doc:
The POSIX errno corresponding to this kind. Lossless round-trip
for all named variants and `.Other`.

members.name: fromErrno
members.doc: Classifies a POSIX errno. Unknown codes fall through to `.Other(c)`.

items.name: brokenPipe
items.doc: `EPIPE` — write to a pipe with no reader.

items.name: invalidInput
items.doc: `EINVAL` — invalid argument to a libc call.

items.name: permissionDenied
items.doc: `EACCES` — caller lacks permission for the operation.
